articles.headline: India hikes export taxes to stabilise jet fuel supply
articles.blurb: India has sharply increased export taxes on aviation turbine fuel (ATF), as the government aims to keep more…...
articles.takeaway: India's export tax increase on ATF signals potential supply constraints that could affect global jet fuel pricing and availability for airlines operating in Asia-Pacific regions.

articles.headline: The Real Reason Why American Airlines Won't Fly Its Boeing 737 MAX 8s Over The Atlantic Or Pacific
articles.blurb: The carrier is the only major US airline to keep its MAX aircraft over land.
articles.takeaway: American Airlines' transatlantic/transpacific MAX 8 restriction reveals persistent airline hesitation about twin-engine ETOPS certification despite FAA approval, signaling operational risk perception beyond regulatory requirements.

articles.headline: 6 Hidden Features Of The Airbus A380 Cabin Layout
articles.blurb: The aircraft certainly is full of surprises.
articles.takeaway: The A380's cabin design includes lesser-known operational efficiencies that maximize passenger comfort and crew workflow beyond standard configurations.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Strong safety reporting cultures directly correlate with reduced accident rates because they enable organizations to identify systemic risks before they cause failures.

articles.headline: 7 Revolutionary Prototype Aircraft That Flew Brilliantly But Never Entered Production
articles.blurb: Revolutionary prototype aircraft can take to the air only to not go into serial production due to a range of factors from obsolescence to bankruptcy.
articles.takeaway: Prototype success doesn't guarantee market viability; economic, regulatory, and technological shifts can render even brilliant designs commercially unviable.

articles.headline: New Raptor: Why America's Top Fighter Is Trading Pure Stealth For Passive Detection & Range
articles.blurb: The US Air Force is upgrading the F-22 Raptor into a 'Raptor 2.0' configuration, adding stealthy external fuel tanks, IRST pods, and avionics upgrades...
articles.takeaway: The F-22's evolution from pure stealth dominance to integrated passive sensing represents a strategic shift toward multi-domain awareness, reflecting lessons learned about modern air combat's emphasis on detection range over signature reduction alone.

articles.headline: Hawaiian Airlines To Permanently Remove Airbus A321neo Flights Between Honolulu & Oakland
articles.blurb: The move will happen this October, boosting capacity by nearly 50%.
articles.takeaway: Hawaiian Airlines' permanent A321neo withdrawal from Honolulu-Oakland signals fleet optimization strategy prioritizing larger-capacity aircraft on this route despite the neo's efficiency advantages.

articles.headline: Not Buying It: Southwest Airlines Criticized After Giving High-Tier Flier A Handwritten Note
articles.blurb: The carrier has sparked an extensive online debate.
articles.takeaway: Southwest's gesture of a handwritten note instead of meaningful compensation reveals how airlines underestimate premium customer expectations during service recovery.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Organizations must document why selected risk controls are appropriate and acceptable, creating an auditable trail that regulators scrutinize beyond the control selection itself.

articles.headline: Why Delta Air Lines Now Only Has 2 Boeing 757 Routes To Europe
articles.blurb: The carrier used to deploy the type on transatlantic duties a lot more widely and regularly.
articles.takeaway: Delta's consolidation of 757 European service reflects strategic fleet optimization toward larger widebody aircraft and reduced transatlantic point-to-point route profitability.

articles.headline: United Airlines Slashes Boeing 737 MAX 8 International Flights By 16%
articles.blurb: The type will, however, see increased usage on European routes.
articles.takeaway: United's selective reduction signals airlines are optimizing 737 MAX deployment by shifting long-haul capacity to higher-margin European markets rather than abandoning the aircraft type.

articles.headline: US Air Force Confronts Range Disadvantage For F-22 & F-35 Missiles Against China's PL-15
articles.blurb: US fighters face a growing missile range gap as China’s PL-15 outpaces AMRAAM, driving urgent development of next-gen weapons like AIM-260.
articles.takeaway: The tactical missile range gap is forcing the US Air Force to accelerate development timelines for next-generation air-to-air weapons, creating budget and integration pressures across fighter platforms.

articles.headline: Battle Scarred Stratotanker Transits Through UK Towards the U.S. for Repairs

articles.headline: Flight Capacity across the Globe in February 2026

articles.blurb: A pay rise equivalent to “a tin of beans” per week, according to trade union Unite, has resulted in a worker’s strike at London Stansted Airport (STN). These workers are part of the team at STN, assisting disabled passengers to their flights. Rejection of pay rise Over 100 of the members that work...
articles.takeaway: Ground service labor disputes over inadequate compensation increases risk operational disruptions at major UK airports and may signal broader industry wage pressure.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

articles.headline: Press Release: Virgin Atlantic accelerates Starlink rollout

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers near-term emissions reductions without requiring new aircraft technology investments.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Gulf Air and Iraqi Airways resumes operations

Gulf Air Resumes Home Base Ops via Riyadh Earlier in March 2026, the...

articles.headline: UK marks 90 years since historic flight of Spitfire aircraft

articles.headline: Dubai Limits Flights Until May 31 Disrupting Indian Airlines

articles.headline: Palma Airport to Introduce Separate Lanes for British Citizens

articles.headline: Dublin Airport warns passengers about protest delays on M50

articles.headline: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS
articles.blurb: A Falcon 9 launched a Cygnus cargo spacecraft April 11 as Northrop Grumman continues its dependence on a competitor to fly resupply missions to the International Space Station.
articles.takeaway: Northrop Grumman's reliance on SpaceX for ISS resupply missions highlights persistent delays in developing its own launch capability alternatives.
